metadata.description: Live test cases for TokenEstimator v1.3. Organized by trap type × surface deception. 9 cells, 3 prompts per cell, 5 runs per prompt = 135 total executions.

matrix.primary_axis: trap_type: none | enumeration | reasoning | fetch | iteration
matrix.secondary_axis: surface_deceptive: false (trap visible in prompt) | true (looks cheap, is expensive)

evaluation_dimensions: estimate_accuracy: did actual token cost fall within the skill's stated band?
trap_identification: did the skill correctly name the trap type(s)?
path_accuracy: did the skill correctly describe the execution path and expensive step?
option_quality: if RISKY, were the scope reduction options concrete with token estimates?

methodology.protocol: same-session — skill estimate first, task execution second
methodology.ground_truth: fully executed — token cost via Anthropic token counting API
methodology.statistical_note: n=5 per prompt is directional. Variance prior from CASTILLO et al. (2025).
methodology.lurking_variable: estimate-priming effect documented but not controlled in this phase

test_cases.cell_note: Control group. Validates skill does not over-flag clean tasks.
test_cases.label: Memo — tool recommendation
test_cases.prompt: Write a one-page memo recommending that our team switch our project management tool from Jira to Linear. Cover ease of use, migration complexity, and cost.
test_cases.notes: Fully self-contained. No fetching, no enumeration. Skill should proceed without flags.

test_cases.label: Python function — running average
test_cases.prompt: Write a Python function that accepts a list of integers and returns a list of running averages. Include docstring and a few example usages.
test_cases.notes: Simplest possible case. Validates no false positive traps on Tier 1 tasks.

test_cases.label: Email — vendor declination
test_cases.prompt: Write a professional email politely declining a vendor's proposal for a new HR software platform. We appreciated the demo but are going in a different direction.
test_cases.notes: Pair with TC-N02 to establish false positive rate baseline for Tier 1.

test_cases.cell_note: Dataset size is stated in the prompt. Skill should route correctly and flag enumeration trap.
test_cases.label: CSV — 500 customer records
test_cases.prompt: I have a CSV file with 500 customer records. Clean it by standardizing all phone numbers to E.164 format and removing any rows where the email field is empty. Return the cleaned CSV.
test_cases.notes: Bounded enumeration — 500 rows stated. Expensive but predictable. Should land SAFE.

test_cases.label: Security review — 12 files
test_cases.prompt: I'm going to paste 12 Python files from our web backend below. Review each one for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and give me a consolidated findings report.
test_cases.notes: File count stated. Input-heavy but bounded. Skill should flag enumeration and size it correctly.

test_cases.label: Price audit — 200 SKUs
test_cases.prompt: I have a list of 200 product SKUs. For each one, look up the current price in our catalog API and flag any that are priced more than 20% below their MSRP.
test_cases.notes: Bounded enumeration + light fetch per item. Count stated so cost is calculable.

test_cases.cell_note: ★ Key stress test. Output is trivial. Dataset is unbounded. Skill must infer enumeration trap from execution path, not prompt surface.
test_cases.label: Gmail — mark old unreads as read
test_cases.prompt: Find all emails in my Gmail that are unread and older than January 1, 2020, and mark them as read.
test_cases.notes: The canonical motivation case. Output is trivial (marking items). Enumeration of a 25-year-old inbox is the cost. Probe should count emails before proceeding.

test_cases.label: CRM — remove duplicate contacts
test_cases.prompt: Go through all the contacts in my HubSpot CRM and remove any duplicates.
test_cases.notes: Same pattern as TC-ED01. Output small, enumeration unbounded. Probe should count contacts.

test_cases.label: Codebase — find all TODO comments
test_cases.prompt: Search through our entire codebase and find every TODO comment, then give me a prioritized list of what to tackle first.
test_cases.notes: Must read all files before any TODOs can be found. Codebase size unknown. Probe should count files.

test_cases.cell_note: Task is openly complex. Long output expected. Skill should flag reasoning depth and size correctly.
test_cases.label: Architecture — microservices decomposition
test_cases.prompt: We're running a Rails monolith and want to decompose it into microservices. Design an optimal microservices architecture for our e-commerce platform, covering domain boundaries, data ownership, communication patterns, and migration sequencing.
test_cases.notes: Openly complex, long output. Skill should flag reasoning depth and possibly recommend Opus.

test_cases.label: Market analysis — 5000-word report
test_cases.prompt: Write a comprehensive 5,000-word analysis of the competitive dynamics in the enterprise SaaS market, covering the major players, pricing trends, consolidation patterns, and where the market is heading over the next 3 years.
test_cases.notes: Reasoning + fetch combo. Output length stated. Should land TIGHT. Tests multi-trap detection.

test_cases.label: Go-to-market strategy — EU expansion
test_cases.prompt: Create a full go-to-market strategy document for launching our B2B SaaS product into the European market. Cover market entry approach, regulatory considerations (GDPR), pricing localization, channel strategy, and a 12-month execution roadmap.
test_cases.notes: Complex but self-contained. No heavy fetching needed. Tests reasoning trap without fetch amplification.

test_cases.cell_note: ★ Key stress test. Output is tiny. Reasoning demand is vast. Skill must flag trap even while returning SAFE on token cost. Estimate accuracy and trap identification diverge here.
test_cases.label: World peace — 500-word brief
test_cases.prompt: Write a 500-word proposal for achieving world peace.
test_cases.notes: The canonical reasoning trap. SAFE on token cost (output constrained) but reasoning demand is vast. Skill should name the trap and note that output constraint limits cost.

test_cases.label: Churn analysis — one paragraph
test_cases.prompt: In one paragraph, explain why our customer churn rate increased 15% last quarter.
test_cases.notes: Looks like a one-liner. Requires causal reasoning across business, product, and market factors. SAFE but trap should be flagged.

test_cases.label: 2008 financial crisis — 3 bullet points
test_cases.prompt: What were the root causes of the 2008 financial crisis? Answer in 3 bullet points.
test_cases.notes: Hardest version of the reasoning/deceptive case. Output extremely constrained. Reasoning depth is high. Tests whether skill flags trap at all when output is Tier 1.

test_cases.cell_note: Source count is stated or clearly implied. Skill should quantify fetch cost from source count.
test_cases.label: Competitor research — 10 companies
test_cases.prompt: Research our top 10 competitors and give me a summary of each covering: pricing model, key features, target customer, and their main differentiator vs. us.
test_cases.notes: 10 companies × multiple pages each. Fetch cost dominates. Skill should quantify: 10 sources × ~2-3K = ~20-30K context cost.

test_cases.label: AI regulation — all G20 countries
test_cases.prompt: Summarize the current state of AI regulation across all G20 countries. For each country, cover: what legislation exists or is in progress, the regulatory body responsible, and the general stance (permissive vs restrictive).
test_cases.notes: 19 countries × multiple regulatory sources. Should trigger RISKY and offer narrowed scope options.

test_cases.label: Cloud database comparison — 8 options
test_cases.prompt: Compare these 8 cloud database options on pricing, scalability, and developer experience: AWS RDS, Aurora, Google Cloud SQL, Firestore, Azure SQL, PlanetScale, Neon, and Supabase.
test_cases.notes: 8 sources, structured comparison. Should land TIGHT. Tests boundary between SAFE and RISKY for fetch tasks.

test_cases.cell_note: ★ Key stress test. Prompt looks like a single question. Correct answer requires many sources. Skill must infer fetch requirement from what a thorough answer would need.
test_cases.label: ORM recommendation — Node.js
test_cases.prompt: What's the best ORM for a Node.js application in 2025? We're using PostgreSQL and need good TypeScript support.
test_cases.notes: Sounds like a quick opinion. A thorough answer requires checking Prisma, Drizzle, TypeORM, MikroORM, Sequelize docs and community sentiment. Skill should infer the fetch requirement.

test_cases.label: React — still worth learning?
test_cases.prompt: Is React still worth learning in 2025, or should I focus on something else?
test_cases.notes: Conversational question. Thorough answer requires checking job market data, framework trend data, community signals. Lighter fetch than TC-FD01 but still present.

test_cases.label: Anthropic model releases — what's new?
test_cases.prompt: What are people saying about Anthropic's latest model releases?
test_cases.notes: Sounds like a simple question. Requires fetching recent news, reviews, benchmarks, community discussions. Tests whether skill infers fetch need from 'what are people saying'.

test_cases.cell_note: No stopping condition stated. Skill should flag iteration trap and probe or ask for constraint.
test_cases.label: API performance — debug until fixed
test_cases.prompt: Our API has been slow for some users but we're not sure why. Debug the performance issue and fix it.
test_cases.notes: Unknown root cause = unknown number of cycles. Probe should assess what diagnostic information is available before committing.

test_cases.label: Test suite — get tests passing
test_cases.prompt: Get all the failing tests in our test suite passing.
test_cases.notes: Number of failures unknown. Each fix may reveal new failures. Probe should count current failures before committing.

test_cases.label: Marketing copy — iterate until great
test_cases.prompt: Here's our current homepage headline and subheadline. Keep refining it until it really sings. [HEADLINE: Build software faster. SUBHEADLINE: The AI-powered development platform for modern teams.]
test_cases.notes: No stopping condition defined. 'Until it really sings' is undefined. Skill should flag and ask user to define done. Lower risk than TC-IO01/02 because output per cycle is small.

test_cases.cell_note: ★ Key stress test. Prompt sounds like a single fix. Skill must recognize hidden iteration potential.
test_cases.label: Flaky test — CI pipeline
test_cases.prompt: There's a flaky test in our CI pipeline that fails about 30% of the time. Fix it.
test_cases.notes: Sounds like one fix. Root cause of flakiness often requires multiple diagnostic cycles (timing issues, shared state, environment variance). Skill should flag iteration risk.

test_cases.label: Product description — improve conversion
test_cases.prompt: Here's our current product description for our flagship plan. Refine it to improve conversion. [DESCRIPTION: Our Professional plan gives your team everything they need to ship faster and collaborate better, starting at $49/month per seat.]
test_cases.notes: 'Improve conversion' has no stopping condition. However output per pass is tiny so total risk is low. Skill should flag the open-endedness while still returning SAFE.

test_cases.label: React component — won't render
test_cases.prompt: My React component isn't rendering correctly — the dropdown menu closes immediately when I click an option. Help me fix it.
test_cases.notes: Sounds like one bug fix. Could be one line or could require multiple diagnostic passes (event propagation, state management, portal rendering). Iteration risk is moderate — skill should note it without blocking.
